# PLAINTIFF CASTILLO MEDICAL TECHNOLOGIES, INC.'S FIRST SET OF INTERROGATORIES TO DEFENDANT VERIDIAN HEALTH SYSTEMS, LLC

 

Pursuant to Rules 26 and 33 of the Federal Rules of Civil Procedure, the Scheduling Order entered by the Court on September 4, 2024, and the Local Rules of the United States District Court for the Western District of Texas, Plaintiff Castillo Medical Technologies, Inc. ("CMT" or "Plaintiff"), by and through its undersigned attorneys, propounds the following First Set of Interrogatories to Defendant Veridian Health Systems, LLC ("Veridian"). Veridian is requested to serve its answers, and any objections, in writing and under oath, within thirty (30) days after service of these interrogatories, pursuant to Fed. R. Civ. P. 33(b)(2).

 

## GENERAL DEFINITIONS

As used in these interrogatories, the following terms have the meanings set forth below:

1. **"CMT"** or **"Plaintiff"** means Castillo Medical Technologies, Inc.

2. **"Veridian"** or **"Defendant"** means Veridian Health Systems, LLC, and its present and former officers, directors, employees, agents, attorneys (as to non-privileged facts), consultants, contractors, subsidiaries, affiliates, successors, and assigns.

3. **"Oshiro"** means Ryan Oshiro.

4. **"CMT Trade Secrets"** means the NeuralPath Algorithm, the PrecisionDrive System, and the PathPlanner Module, as those terms are defined below and as used in CMT's First Amended Complaint, together with all related source code, CAD schematics, engineering drawings, training datasets, model weights, design specifications, control algorithms, calibration protocols, and associated know-how and documentation.

5. **"NeuralPath Algorithm"** means CMT's proprietary real-time intraoperative neural mapping algorithm, including its source code, signal processing pipeline, latency optimization modules, and calibration procedures.

6. **"PrecisionDrive System"** means CMT's proprietary micro-actuation system for sub-millimeter robotic arm control, including its harmonic drive configuration, CAD schematics, mechanical designs, control algorithms, and engineering tolerances.

7. **"PathPlanner Module"** means CMT's proprietary machine-learning-based surgical planning module, including its training datasets, model weights, model architectures, hyperparameters, and output calibration methods.

8. **"SynapticEdge"** means Veridian's SynapticEdge™ surgical robotics platform, announced on June 11, 2024, including all features, components, subsystems, software, firmware, hardware, and documentation thereof, in any version or iteration.

9. **"VaultDrive"** means CMT's secure, access-controlled engineering repository.

10. **"USB Drive"** means the 2TB Corsair portable external USB storage device bearing serial number CX-8827491, and any copy, clone, forensic image, backup, or derivative thereof.

11. **"NDA"** means the Confidentiality, Non-Disclosure, and Invention Assignment Agreement between CMT and Oshiro dated March 5, 2018.

12. **"CMT Confidential Information"** means any information constituting "Confidential Information" as defined in Section 1.1 of the NDA.

13. **"The Three Engineers"** means Dr. Kenji Furukawa, Samantha Briggs, and David Moreno.

14. **"Person"** means any individual, proprietorship, firm, partnership, joint venture, corporation, limited liability company, association, or other entity.

15. **"Communication"** means any oral, written, or electronic exchange of information, including but not limited to emails, text messages, instant messages, LinkedIn messages, Slack messages, Microsoft Teams messages, voicemails, letters, memoranda, and conversations.

16. **"Identify,"** when used with reference to a Person, means to state the Person's full name, present or last-known business and residential addresses, telephone number, present employer and position, and any position held with Veridian or CMT and the dates thereof.

17. **"Identify,"** when used with reference to a document, means to state its type (e.g., email, contract, memorandum), title or description, date, author(s), recipient(s), custodian(s), and present location or where a copy may be obtained.

18. **"Identify,"** when used with reference to a Communication, means to state its date, form, all participants, and the subject matter and substance of the Communication.

19. **"Document"** means any written, printed, typed, graphic, recorded, or electronically stored matter of any kind, however produced or reproduced, including emails, text messages, instant messages, Slack or Teams messages, voicemails, letters, memoranda, reports, contracts, presentations, spreadsheets, source code, CAD files, design documents, project plans, meeting notes, and audio or video recordings.

 

## GENERAL INSTRUCTIONS

A. Veridian is requested to furnish full, complete, direct, and unequivocal answers to each interrogatory, based upon all information known to or reasonably available to Veridian, including information in the possession, custody, or control of Veridian's present and former officers, directors, employees, agents, attorneys (as to non-privileged facts), consultants, contractors, and accountants. If an answer requires information not known to Veridian but reasonably available to it, Veridian shall make reasonable inquiry to obtain the information.

B. If Veridian objects to any interrogatory or subpart thereof, Veridian shall state the specific grounds for the objection with particularity, pursuant to Fed. R. Civ. P. 33(b)(4), and shall nevertheless answer the interrogatory to the fullest extent the objection does not preclude an answer. A general or boilerplate objection shall not be deemed a proper response.

C. If Veridian cannot answer an interrogatory in full, Veridian shall answer to the extent possible, state the information that is available, and explain why a fuller answer cannot be furnished.

D. If any information responsive to an interrogatory is or has been withheld on the basis of a claim of privilege or other protection, Veridian shall provide a privilege log identifying each withheld document or Communication with sufficient detail to permit CMT to assess the claim, consistent with Fed. R. Civ. P. 26(b)(5) and any applicable protective order.

E. Veridian's answers shall be verified by an officer or managing agent of Veridian, or by such other person as may be permitted under Fed. R. Civ. P. 33(b)(5).

F. These interrogatories are continuing in nature. Veridian is obligated to supplement its answers pursuant to Fed. R. Civ. P. 26(e) as additional or corrective information becomes known or available.

G. Veridian may designate any answer or portion thereof as "Confidential" or "Attorneys' Eyes Only" under the Protective Order entered in this action, provided that Veridian complies with the procedures set forth in the Protective Order for such designations.

H. The subparts of each interrogatory are intended to be, and shall be construed as, components of a single, unified inquiry into the subject matter of that interrogatory.

A court-ready **First Set of Interrogatories** from Plaintiff Castillo Medical Technologies, Inc. to Defendant Veridian Health Systems, LLC in Civil Action No. 1:24-cv-03841-RLH (W.D. Tex.).

### How I approached it
I read all nine case files to extract the operative facts and procedural constraints, then drafted interrogatories tailored to the claims and defenses:

- **Scheduling Order** drove the key constraints: a strict **25-interrogatory cap** (with discrete subparts counted separately unless part of a single unified inquiry), a **Jan. 2, 2025** service deadline, and a **proportionality directive** focusing discovery on the three identified trade secrets.
- **Complaint/NDA/TRO/forensics/expert reports** supplied the factual targets: Oshiro's recruitment by CEO Marcus Trent, the 4,700-file/38.4 GB exfiltration to USB drive CX-8827491, the 85-day SynapticEdge timeline, the five overlapping technical features, and the solicitation of three CMT engineers.

### Document contents
- Caption, Rule 33 service/answer notice
- 19 definitions and 8 instructions (including a statement that subparts are unified inquiries)
- **25 interrogatories** organized by topic: recruitment of Oshiro (1–5), CMT files/USB drive at Veridian (6–9), SynapticEdge development (10–17), solicitation of CMT engineers (18–20), commercial exploitation/damages (21–22), and defenses/general (23–25)
- Signature block and certificate of service

The .docx passes the ECMA-376 schema and ZIP-integrity validation gate, and all 25 interrogatories render correctly.
